descriptions: SGDs and Insane Two One Boys became friends through incarceration.
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 80s, members of the SGDs were incarcerated with members of the Insane Two One Boys, notably a Two One elder named Pepe, this brought a friendship with the two organizations that carried out to the streets.

descriptions: Bruno Giugliano and Salvatore Notaro were considered referents for both the Cava and Ruocco-Somma clans, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: ritenuti i referenti nel Nolano dei clan "Cava" di Quindici e "Ruocco-Somma" di Piazzolla di Nola

descriptions: The Lubrano family was very close to the Orlando clan, with Lubrano sons being trusted by boss Antonio Orlando.
edges.evidence_quote: Oltre ai rapporti storici con i Nuvoletta, la famiglia Lubrano è stata anche molto vicina agli Orlando , e secondo le indagini, i figli di Gaetano Lubrano, Armando, Raffaele e Vincenzo, sarebbero i principali fiduciari del boss Antonio Orlando.

descriptions: Orlando replaced Clan Nuvoletta and has strong family ties to it.
edges.evidence_quote: il clan è stato sostituito sul territorio dai Polverino e Orlando , entrambi con forti legami di parentela con i Nuvoletta.

descriptions: The Orlando clan succeeded the Nuvoletta clan after its decline.
edges.evidence_quote: the clan was succeeded by the Polverino and Orlando clans

descriptions: Polverino clan is allied with Orlando clan per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Nuvoletta clan, Orlando clan, Mallardo clan, Lubrano-Ligato clan, Licciardi clan, Cosa Nostra

descriptions: Salakau routinely attacked Sakongsa as a rival gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Attacks on rival gangs such as Sakongsa, Omega and 18 Sio Yi Ho were somewhat of a routine occurrence.

descriptions: Galloping Goose expanded into territory formerly controlled by Pharaohs, indicating a conflict over territory.
edges.evidence_quote: An expert on outlaw motorcycle gangs from Missouri State Highway Patrol said the Galloping Goose were expanding into territory formerly controlled by the Pharaohs motorcycle club during the 1980s and 1990s.

descriptions: Crazy Dragons succeeded the Trang gang after it folded.
edges.evidence_quote: The Crazy Dragons were the successor gang to the Vietnamese Trang gang, which folded after a series of convictions of its members in 2002.

descriptions: Sahara Emirate Brigade was a group within AQIM until 2017.
edges.evidence_quote: Groups: ... Sahara Emirate Brigade (until 2017)

United Wa State Army.description: The United Wa State Army is the military wing of the United Wa State Party, the de facto ruling party of Wa State in Myanmar. Formed in 1989 after the collapse of the Communist Party of Burma, it is a well-equipped ethnic minority army of 20,000–30,000 soldiers led by Bao Youxiang. Despite being de facto independent, it officially recognizes Myanmar's sovereignty and has signed ceasefire and peace deals. It is the largest non-state armed group in Myanmar and leads the Federal Political Negotiation and Consultative Committee.

descriptions: UWSA split from CPB after internal mutiny.
edges.evidence_quote: Following the CPB's internal mutiny on 16 April 1989, ethnic Wa cadres under the command of Bao Youxiang announced their split from the party and the founding of the United Wa State Party and United Wa State Army

descriptions: The Red Wa are associated with the United Wa State Army.
edges.evidence_quote: They are known for cooperating with the Red Wa (who are associated with the United Wa State Army) for the trafficking and sale of narcotics.

descriptions: United Wa State Army is listed as an ally of Sam Gor in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 14K, Bamboo Union, Big Circle Gang, Sun Yee On, Wo Shing Wo, Yamaguchi-gumi, United Wa State Army, Lebanese mafia, Comancheros, Hells Angels

descriptions: TNLA received public support from United Wa State Army.
edges.evidence_quote: the TNLA received public support from other rebel groups like the KIA and United Wa State Army

descriptions: UWSA formed after CPB's armed wing collapsed.
edges.evidence_quote: The UWSA was formed after the collapse of the armed wing of the Communist Party of Burma (CPB) in 1989.

descriptions: UWSA formed after CPB's collapse.
edges.evidence_quote: The UWSA was formed after the collapse of the armed wing of the Communist Party of Burma (CPB) in 1989.

descriptions: UWSA is allied with Karenni National People's Liberation Front.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Non-state allies ... Karenni National People's Liberation Front

descriptions: UWSA was in conflict with Mong Tai Army until 1996.
edges.evidence_quote: Until 1996 the United Wa State Army was involved in a conflict against the Shan Mong Tai Army led by drug kingpin Khun Sa

descriptions: UWSA has close links with MNDAA.
edges.evidence_quote: the UWSA maintains close links with FPNCC partners like the Myanmar National Democratic Alliance Army (MNDAA)

descriptions: UWSA is allied with NDAA.
edges.evidence_quote: it serves as a link with its ally, the National Democratic Alliance Army (NDAA) in Mongla

descriptions: UWSA fights against Shan State Army (RCSS).
edges.evidence_quote: the Tatmadaw (Myanmar Armed Forces) has frequently allied with the UWSA to fight against Shan nationalist militia groups, such as the Shan State Army (RCSS)

descriptions: UWSA allied with Tatmadaw to fight against Shan State Army.
edges.evidence_quote: the Tatmadaw (Myanmar Armed Forces) has frequently allied with the UWSA to fight against Shan nationalist militia groups, such as the Shan State Army (RCSS).

descriptions: UWSA stopped providing weapons to Shan State Army - North in 2025.
edges.evidence_quote: the UWSA declared that they would no longer provide weapons or financial aid to the TNLA, MNDAA, and Shan State Army - North

descriptions: UWSA opposed Shan State National Army until 2005.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Non-state opponents ... Shan State National Army (until 2005)

descriptions: UWSA has close links with Ta'ang Army.
edges.evidence_quote: the UWSA maintains close links with FPNCC partners like ... the Ta'ang Army

descriptions: UWSA stopped providing weapons to TNLA in 2025.
edges.evidence_quote: the UWSA declared that they would no longer provide weapons or financial aid to the TNLA, MNDAA, and Shan State Army - North

descriptions: UWSA works with ULFA-ATF.
edges.evidence_quote: The United Wa State Army also works with the ULFA-ATF, an Assam resistance group

descriptions: UWSA and UWSP were founded together as twin organizations.
edges.evidence_quote: ethnic Wa cadres under the command of Bao Youxiang announced their split from the party and the founding of the United Wa State Party and United Wa State Army

descriptions: UWSA is the military wing of UWSP.
edges.evidence_quote: The United Wa State Army ... is the military wing of the United Wa State Party (UWSP)

descriptions: UWSA opposed Wa National Army until 2007.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Non-state opponents ... Wa National Army (until 2007)

descriptions: The text states the Australian Bandidos are allied with the Mobshitters.
edges.evidence_quote: The Australian Bandidos are allied with the Diablos , Mobshitters and Rock Machine

Grupo Flechas.description: Armed wing of the Cabrera Sarabia brothers' La Mayiza faction of the Sinaloa Cartel, created in 2020 by Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada to counter the Jalisco New Generation Cartel. Operates in multiple Mexican states, engaging in drug trafficking, homicide, arms trafficking, and other crimes. After Zambada's arrest in 2024, leadership passed to his son Ismael Zambada Sicairos. The group is known for using paramilitary tactics and heavy weaponry.

descriptions: Infobox lists Grupo Flechas as an enemy of CJNG.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: ... Grupo Flechas, Cártel del Noreste, Cártel de Guasave, Cártel Nueva Plaza, Guerreros Unidos, Artistas Asesinos, Cártel de Santa Rosa de Lima, La Familia Michoacana, Los Caballeros Templarios, Los Talibanes, Unión Tepito, Cártel de los Soles, Los Choneros

descriptions: Grupo Flechas captured CJNG members.
edges.evidence_quote: On 23 January 2025, Grupo Flechas, aligned with the Mayitos, were reported to have captured CJNG members in Villanueva, Zacatecas.

edges.source: Cártel de Santa Rosa de Lima
descriptions: CSRL is allied with Grupo Flechas according to infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Grupo Escorpión , Grupo Flechas

descriptions: FEU and Grupo Flechas are on opposing sides in clashes.
edges.evidence_quote: On 17 December 2025, intense clashes were reported in Escuinapa, Sinaloa, between rival armed groups, likely involving Fuerzas Especiales Unión (FEU)— CJNG and Los Chapitos—against forces of "Grupo Flechas" of Mayito Flaco (MZ/MF).

descriptions: Grupo Flechas's main mission is to stop the advance of CJNG.
edges.evidence_quote: su misión principal consiste en frenar el avance del Cártel de Jalisco Nueva Generación

edges.target: Cártel de Santa Rosa de Lima
descriptions: Infobox lists Santa Rosa de Lima as an ally of Grupo Flechas.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Cártel de Santa Rosa de Lima

descriptions: Grupo Flechas is aligned with La Mayiza and acts on its behalf.
edges.evidence_quote: Mayito Flaco reportedly authorized Flechas-aligned forces to expand into southern Sinaloa, directly challenging Chapitos control

descriptions: Grupo Flechas is the armed wing of the La Mayiza faction.
edges.evidence_quote: El Grupo Flechas también conocido como Operativa MZ/MF , es un brazo armado de los hermanos Cabrera Sarabia de Durango de la facción La Mayiza del Cártel de Sinaloa

descriptions: Infobox lists Cartel del Noreste as an ally of Grupo Flechas.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Cártel del Noreste

descriptions: Grupo Flechas provides weapons and funding to Cartel Nueva Plaza.
edges.evidence_quote: el Grupo Flechas le ha otorgado apoyo armamentistico y financiamiento a un grupo local denominado Cártel Nueva Plaza

descriptions: Grupo Flechas is an armed wing of the Sinaloa Cartel's La Mayiza faction.
edges.evidence_quote: El Grupo Flechas también conocido como Operativa MZ/MF , es un brazo armado de los hermanos Cabrera Sarabia de Durango de la facción La Mayiza del Cártel de Sinaloa

descriptions: Grupo Flechas is affiliated with MZ/MF.
edges.evidence_quote: On 9 September 2025, MF/MF-affiliated Grupo Flechas sicarios were documented on the streets of Pánuco, Sinaloa.

descriptions: Grupo Flechas is a sub-group or ally of Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: the cartel's sub-groups and allies are known to consist of ... Grupo Flechas

Stidda.description: The Stidda is a Sicilian Mafia-type criminal organization based in southern Sicily, founded in the early 1980s by former Cosa Nostra members expelled during the Second Mafia War. It operates primarily in rural areas and is known for its star tattoo. The Stidda engaged in a violent conflict with Cosa Nostra in the 1990s, resulting in over 300 deaths. It remains active, with an estimated 2,000 full members.

descriptions: The Milanese faction of Cursoti cooperated with the Stidda in arms and drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 17 ottobre 1993 l'inchiesta del GICO della Guardia di Finanza , coordinata dalla Direzione Distrettuale Antimafia di Firenze , portò all'arresto di Jimmy Miano ed altri appartenenti alla banda dei Cursoti milanesi, che avevano la loro base operativa presso l'autoparco di via Oreste Salomone a Milano , dove gestivano imponenti traffici di armi e droga insieme ad esponenti della Stidda di Gela

descriptions: Nuova Mala del Brenta colluded with Stidda members in fraudulent scams.
edges.evidence_quote: It is also known that Brenta mafiosi have colluded with Stidda members on a number of occasions involving a number of fraudulent scams at a Maltese casino in 2002.

descriptions: Stidda and the Cappello and Laudani clans have rivaled Cosa Nostra for decades and now exert influence over it in drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: nella parte centrale ed orientale dell'isola prevale invece una struttura di tipo orizzontale, non più verticistica (« ad assetto variabile », l'ha definita un rapporto della DIA ), sul modello della Stidda e dei clan Cappello e Laudani , che ormai, dopo decenni di rivalità, esercitano un'enorme influenza su Cosa nostra, specialmente nel campo del traffico di stupefacenti

descriptions: Cosa Nostra fought against Stidda in a violent conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Tra il 1986 e il 1991, Cosa nostra si trovò a fronteggiare una nuova organizzazione criminale, la cosiddetta "Stidda"

descriptions: Cosa Nostra and Stidda agreed to a truce and peaceful coexistence.
edges.evidence_quote: Cosa nostra rischiò di soccombere nello scontro e nel 1991 si trovò costretta a stipulare un patto di "non belligeranza" con gli stiddari ribelli, che prevedeva una ripartizione dei proventi illeciti in parti uguali tra le due organizzazioni, che da allora hanno iniziato una pacifica convivenza durata fino ad oggi.

descriptions: Stidda is allied with 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 'Ndrangheta, Camorra, Cosa Nostra

descriptions: Stidda countered Cosa Nostra's power and expansion, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: The Cosa Nostra wars of the late 1970s and early 1980s brought the Corleonesi clan and its vicious and ruthless leaders ... into power. This caused disorganization and disenchantment inside the traditional Cosa Nostra power base and values system, leaving the growing Stidda organization to counter Cosa Nostra's power, influence, and expansion in Southern and Eastern Sicily.

descriptions: Stidda is a rival and dissident offshoot of Cosa Nostra.
edges.evidence_quote: It is most active in the rural parts of southern Sicily and is a rival to the Cosa Nostra, originating as a dissident offshoot group of the Sicilian Cosa Nostra.

descriptions: Stidda and Cosa Nostra were rivals until the end of the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Cosa Nostra (until the end of 1990s)

descriptions: Stidda is allied with Cosa Nostra (infobox lists as ally despite rivalry).
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 'Ndrangheta, Camorra, Cosa Nostra

descriptions: The text compares Stidda to Cosa Nostra, describing Stidda as weaker and less structured.
edges.evidence_quote: Rispetto a Cosa nostra , la Stidda è molto più debole, meno strutturata, alquanto frammentaria, ma anche radicata, specie in alcune zone dove Cosa nostra è tradizionalmente più debole.

descriptions: Stidda is allied with Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 'Ndrangheta, Camorra, Cosa Nostra

descriptions: Stidda established important relations with the Neapolitan Camorra for drug supplies.
edges.evidence_quote: le recenti operazioni di polizia hanno dimostrato che il clan gelese della Stidda ha stabilito importanti relazioni con il clan Cappello di Catania e con la camorra napoletana per imponenti forniture di cocaina , marijuana e hashish

descriptions: Stidda established important relations with the Cappello clan for drug supplies.
edges.evidence_quote: le recenti operazioni di polizia hanno dimostrato che il clan gelese della Stidda ha stabilito importanti relazioni con il clan Cappello di Catania e con la camorra napoletana per imponenti forniture di cocaina , marijuana e hashish

descriptions: The Corleonesi exterminated the Trapani Stidda faction.
edges.evidence_quote: Nei primi anni 1990 ad Alcamo e Marsala i Corleonesi di Totò Riina , Leoluca Bagarella , Giovanni Brusca e Matteo Messina Denaro , che sterminarono la stidda trapanese dei Greco-Zichittella

'Ndrina Bruzzaniti.description: The Bruzzaniti are a 'ndrina (clan) of the 'Ndrangheta criminal organization, originating from Africo, Calabria, Italy. They are active in the Milan area and are allied with the Morabito, Palamara, and Crisafulli clans. The article provides a brief overview of their history from the 1970s to the 2020s.

descriptions: Bruzzaniti is a 'ndrina (subgroup) of the 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: I Bruzzaniti sono una 'ndrina della 'ndrangheta originaria di Africo.

descriptions: Bruzzaniti are allies of the Crisafulli.
edges.evidence_quote: alleati dei ... Crisafulli

descriptions: Bruzzaniti are allies of the Morabito.
edges.evidence_quote: alleati dei Morabito

descriptions: Bruzzaniti are allies of the Palamara.
edges.evidence_quote: alleati dei ... Palamara

descriptions: Bruzzaniti (as part of Morabito-Bruzzaniti-Palamara) trafficked drugs with the Casalesi Camorra clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Inoltre trafficavano in droga con la cosca camorrista dei casalesi.

descriptions: Bruzzaniti, Palamara, and Morabito trafficked drugs with the Casalesi Camorra clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Inoltre trafficavano in droga con la cosca camorristica dei casalesi .

descriptions: The Mazzaferro have contacts with the Bruzzaniti.
edges.evidence_quote: ma hanno contatti anche con i Barbaro , i Calabrò, i Bruzzaniti , i Morabito e i Raso-Albanese

descriptions: Bruzzaniti are main allies of Morabito in Milan.
edges.evidence_quote: A Milano i principali alleati dei Morabito sono i Bruzzaniti e i Palamara

descriptions: Morabito is allied with Bruzzaniti in Milan.
edges.evidence_quote: In Milan, the Morabito 'ndrina main allies are the Bruzzaniti and Palamara 'ndrine

descriptions: Palamara is allied with Bruzzaniti.
edges.evidence_quote: I Palamara o Palamara-Staiti sono una 'ndrina di Africo alleata dei Bruzzaniti e dei Morabito .

New Zealand Nomads.description: The New Zealand Nomads are a large organized criminal gang formed in Horowhenua, New Zealand in 1977 by Dennis Hines. Originally members of Black Power, they split to form their own gang. The gang is involved in drug dealing, theft, murder, and extortion, and has a long-standing rivalry with the Mongrel Mob. As of 2013, they had 103 members in prison.

descriptions: Bandidos and Nomads jointly conducted a kidnapping in 2006.
edges.evidence_quote: The kidnapping was allegedly a joint Bandidos and Nomads operation, as Sande was also affiliated with the Nomads.

descriptions: Infobox lists New Zealand Nomads as an ally of Black Power.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Crips , Killa Beez (gang) , King Cobras (gang) New Zealand Nomads

descriptions: Blue Angels and Nomads were in a turf war starting after Blue Angels expanded into Aberdeenshire.
edges.evidence_quote: A turf war involving the Blue Angels and the Nomads – a motorcycle club formed in Aberdeen in 1966 – started after the Blue Angels expanded into Aberdeenshire

descriptions: DLASTHR is suspected of killing a former Nomads member.
edges.evidence_quote: ex- Nomads bikie gang member Adrian Buxton was fatally shot outside his home on 17 May 2016. In the aftermath of Hermiz’s killing, police came to suspect that the Assyrian criminal organisation may have been behind Buxton’s death

descriptions: Head Hunters are allies with Nomads.
edges.evidence_quote: the Nomads

descriptions: Hells Angels in conflict with Nomads.
edges.evidence_quote: while they have been involved in conflicts with the Bandidos , Comancheros , Diablos , Finks , Nomads

descriptions: Hells Angels are allied with Nomads in Australia.
edges.evidence_quote: In Australia, they have 150-250 members and are allied with the Nomads.

descriptions: Highway 61 members murdered a member of the New Zealand Nomads, leading to convictions.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1997, Highway 61 members were convicted of murdering a member of the New Zealand Nomads.

descriptions: Infobox lists Black Power as an ally of Nomads.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Black Power, Head Hunters

descriptions: Nomads originated as a faction of Black Power before splitting off.
edges.evidence_quote: The Nomads were originally members of the Black Power, known as the "Black Power Nomads", before a large portion of the gang split and formed their own gang in 1977.

descriptions: Infobox lists Head Hunters as an ally of Nomads.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Black Power, Head Hunters

descriptions: Highway 61 gang killed a Nomad member during a conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1997, tensions heated up with the Highway 61 gang and resulted in the murder of Nomad Malcom Munns.

descriptions: Infobox lists Mongrel Mob as a rival of Nomads.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mongrel Mob

descriptions: Listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bad Troublesome Ward, New Zealand Nomads , Black Power

descriptions: MNDAA succeeded CPB control in Kokang after CPB disbanded.
edges.evidence_quote: From the 1960s to 1989, the Kokang area in northern Shan state was controlled by the Communist Party of Burma, and after the party's armed wing disbanded in 1989 it became a special region of Myanmar under the control of the Myanmar Nationalities Democratic Alliance Army (MNDAA)

descriptions: People's Liberation Army is the armed wing of the Communist Party of Burma.
edges.evidence_quote: The Communist Party of Burma rearmed itself and announced the creation of its new armed wing, the People's Liberation Army, in late 2021.

descriptions: UWSA split from CPB after internal mutiny.
edges.evidence_quote: Following the CPB's internal mutiny on 16 April 1989, ethnic Wa cadres under the command of Bao Youxiang announced their split from the party and the founding of the United Wa State Party and United Wa State Army

descriptions: NDAA is a splinter group that broke away from CPB.
edges.evidence_quote: A splinter group led by Sai Leun which broke away from the CPB at the same time established the National Democratic Alliance Army (NDAA)

descriptions: SSA and CPB were in conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: In northern Shan State, the SSA came into conflict with the Communist Party of Burma (CPB)

descriptions: UWSA formed after CPB's armed wing collapsed.
edges.evidence_quote: The UWSA was formed after the collapse of the armed wing of the Communist Party of Burma (CPB) in 1989.

descriptions: UWSA formed after CPB's collapse.
edges.evidence_quote: The UWSA was formed after the collapse of the armed wing of the Communist Party of Burma (CPB) in 1989.

descriptions: PLA collaborated with ANM to establish Heroes of Return.
edges.evidence_quote: In collaboration with the Palestine Liberation Organisation's (PLO) Palestinian Liberation Army (PLA), the ANM established the Abtal al-Audah (Heroes of Return) commando group in early summer 1966.

descriptions: The Order distributed robbery proceeds to the National Alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: Proceeds from these robberies were distributed to leaders of sympathetic organizations such as William Pierce (National Alliance) and Frazier Glenn Miller, Jr. (White Patriot Party).

descriptions: Jund al-Aqsa is listed as an opponent since October 2016.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Jund al-Aqsa ( since October 2016 )

descriptions: Ahrar al-Sham attended a meeting with Jund al-Aqsa to unite against Syrian government.
edges.evidence_quote: In early November 2014, representatives from Ahrar al-Sham reportedly attended a meeting with al-Nusra Front, the Khorasan Group, the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant, and Jund al-Aqsa, which sought to unite the groups against the Syrian government.

descriptions: Al-Nusra was allied with Jund al-Aqsa.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Jund al-Aqsa

descriptions: Jund Al-Aqsa fighters joined Al-Nusra.
edges.evidence_quote: On 17 February 2016, nearly half of Jund Al-Aqsa, numbering 400 fighters, joined Al-Nusra.

descriptions: Jund Al-Aqsa merged with Jabhat Fatah Al-Sham.
edges.evidence_quote: On 9 October, Jund Al-Aqsa, after infighting between them and numerous rebel groups including Ahrar Al-Sham, merged with JFS.

descriptions: Black Gaza lists SouthPark Freestone Blood Gang as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Cashion Park , SouthPark Freestone Blood Gang

descriptions: Cohen raised funds for Irgun.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1947 Ben Hecht of the Zionist American League for a Free Palestine approached Cohen asking for his help to raise funds for the Jewish paramilitary group Irgun. Cohen agreed and a fundraiser was held at his Slapsy Maxie's Café in LA, reportedly raising $200,000
